de.uka.ilkd.key.speclang

Class StatementWellDefinedness

  • java.lang.Object
    • de.uka.ilkd.key.speclang.WellDefinednessCheck
      • de.uka.ilkd.key.speclang.StatementWellDefinedness
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    Contract, SpecificationElement
    Direct Known Subclasses:
    BlockWellDefinedness, LoopWellDefinedness

    public abstract class StatementWellDefinedness
extends WellDefinednessCheck
    A contract for checking the well-definedness of a jml statement. Contrary to jml specifications for methods, model fields and class invariants, the well-definedness of jml statements is checked "on-the-fly", i.e. before/when it is applied, since the check needs the context it is placed in. Thus the technical difference to other well-definedness checks is the context update and that it is not a separate contract, but another branch of the specific rule of the overlying proof. The according proof sequent is built in generateSequent(ProgramVariable, ProgramVariable, ProgramVariable, LocationVariable, ProgramVariable, Term, ImmutableSet, Term, Services) Nevertheless it is imaginable to make them separate contracts.
    Author:
    Michael Kirsten

    • Nested Class Summary

      Nested Classes 
      Modifier and Type Class and Description
      (package private) class  StatementWellDefinedness.SequentTerms
      A data structure to pass the needed terms for the well-definedness sequent of a jml statement, including the context update, pre-condition for the statement, well-formedness condition for the anonymous heap, well-definedness term for the statement's assignable-clause, well-definedness term for other clauses in the statement and the well-definedness term for the statement's post-condition with the according updates (heap of pre-state becomes current heap and the current heap gets anonymised) applied to it.

      • Nested classes/interfaces inherited from class de.uka.ilkd.key.speclang.WellDefinednessCheck

        WellDefinednessCheck.Condition, WellDefinednessCheck.POTerms, WellDefinednessCheck.TermAndFunc, WellDefinednessCheck.Type

      • Nested classes/interfaces inherited from interface de.uka.ilkd.key.speclang.Contract

        Contract.OriginalVariables

      • createSeqTerms

        final StatementWellDefinedness.SequentTerms createSeqTerms(WellDefinednessCheck.POTerms po,
                                                           WellDefinednessPO.Variables vars,
                                                           Term leadingUpdate,
                                                           Services services)
        Aggregates and preprocesses the proof obligation data into the according terms for the well-definedness sequent of the jml statement.
        Parameters:
        po - the proof obligation terms of the statement
        vars - the new (current) variables
        leadingUpdate - the context update of the program before the statement
        services -
        Returns:
        the actual terms used in the well-definedness sequent

      • generateSequent

        public SequentFormula generateSequent(ProgramVariable self,
                                      ProgramVariable exception,
                                      ProgramVariable result,
                                      LocationVariable heap,
                                      ProgramVariable heapAtPre,
                                      Term anonHeap,
                                      ImmutableSet<ProgramVariable> ps,
                                      Term leadingUpdate,
                                      Services services)
        This is where the proof sequent is built.
        Parameters:
        self - The current self variable
        exception - The current exception variable
        result - The current result variable
        heap - The current heap
        heapAtPre - The current old heap
        anonHeap - The anonymized heap
        ps - The current parameter variables
        leadingUpdate - The context update
        services - The current services reference
        Returns:
        The proof sequent for the well-definedness check

      • generateSequent

        public SequentFormula generateSequent(ProgramVariable self,
                                      LocationVariable heap,
                                      Term anonHeap,
                                      ImmutableSet<ProgramVariable> ps,
                                      Term leadingUpdate,
                                      Services services)
        This is where the proof sequent is built, however with a smaller set of variables, due to the nature of the jml statement. This means no exception variable, no result variable and variable for the heap of the pre-state.
        Parameters:
        self - self variable
        heap - heap variable
        anonHeap - anonymised heap
        ps - set of parameter variables
        leadingUpdate - the context update
        services -
        Returns:
        The proof seuqne t for the well-definedness check
